content.problemStatement:
Let's set up the basic Django project, create the comments app, and model/display the static pages that comments will be attached to.

The project, app and the required files (mostly empty) are created for you. Check out the file explorer to see the files and directories. You will see how they are used in each of the steps.

**Project:** `comment_system` \
**App:** `comments` 
- **Note 1:** Each time you click **RUN** in this project, a superuser account with the following credentials will be created for you to make the testing easier: \
**Username:** `chef` \
**Password:** `chefpass@123`

- **Note 2:** A set of static blog pages will be loaded via fixtures each time you **RUN** so that we have some blog pages to work on. This is achieved using the the file `comment_system/pages.json`

### Files to Create/Modify for this Task:
- comment_system/settings.py 
   - Register your app and configure static files. (Line #30, #103, #104)
- comment_system/urls.py 
   - Wire up your app's URLs to the main project.
- comments/models.py  
   - Define the model for the static pages (or "blog posts").
- comments/admin.py (Done for you)
- comments/views.py 
   - Create the views to display the list of pages and a single page.
- comments/urls.py (Done for you)
- comments/templates/comments/base.html (Done for you)
- comments/templates/comments/homepage.html (Done for you)
- comments/templates/comments/page_detail.html (Done for you)
- comments/static/comments/style.css (Done for you)

**After completing the above**:
- Click on **RUN** to start the server. Open the browser on a new tab.
- You will see a homepage listing several "blog post" titles. 
- Clicking a title will take you to a detail page showing that specific post's content. 
- There will be no users or comment functionality yet—this task is all about building the foundation.
